---
title: "1.1 Introduction to Algorithms, Programming, and Compilers"
subtitle: "Part 3: Runtime Errors and Debugging Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Run-Time Errors

### Errors after compiling

Some errors cannot be detected by the compiler. These are called <span class="term">run-time errors</span> (运行时错误).

These errors occur when the program is running, after the code is compiled.

They can be caused by a variety of things, such as dividing by zero or trying to read from a file that does not exist or a logic error in the code.

## Exceptions

### Unexpected run-time errors

An <span class="term">exception</span> is a type of run-time error that occurs as a result of an unexpected error that was not detected by the compiler.

It interrupts the normal flow of the program's execution.

Java will sometimes report an exception with a message that tells you what went wrong.

## Code Task

### `activecode:: divideByZeroDemo`

Textbook prompt: The following code has a run-time error.

When you click run, the compiler will not catch it since it is not a syntax error.

The program will run and try to compute `3` divided by `0`, which cannot be computed.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: divideByZeroDemo`

```java
public class DivideByZero
{
   public static void main(String[] args)
   {
       System.out("It makes no sense to divide a number by zero!");
       System.out.println(3/0);
   }
}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: divideByZeroDemo`

Runestone checks that the code contains:

```text
DivideByZero
```

The textbook purpose is to observe that the program reports an <span class="term">ArithmeticException</span> while running.

## Logic Errors

### Runs, but behaves incorrectly

Run-time errors can be difficult to find because they do not always cause the program to crash.

Sometimes they just cause the program to behave in unexpected ways because of an error in the logic.

A <span class="term">logic error</span> is a mistake in the algorithm or program that causes it to behave incorrectly or unexpectedly.

Logic errors can be detected by testing the program with specific data to see if it produces the expected outcome.

## Comments

### Notes skipped by the compiler

In Java and many text-based coding languages, `//` is used to mark the beginning of a comment.

For multi-line comments, use `/*` to start the comment and `*/` to end the comment.

The compiler will skip over comments.

## Comment Examples

### Good commenting

```java
/* MyClass.java
   Programmer: My Name
   Date:
*/

int max = 10; // this keeps track of the max score
```

It is a good idea to use comments to make notes to yourself and other programmers working with you.

## Groupwork Debugging Challenge

### Rubber duck debugging

::: {.image-fit}
![Rubber Duck](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-1-introduction-to-algorithms-programming-and-compilers/rubberduck.jpg){fig-align="center" width="20%"}
:::

In this course, you are encouraged to work together in pairs to complete the coding challenges.

If you are working alone, explain the code to a rubber duck or another toy.

## Pair Programming

### Driver and navigator

Pair programming is a technique where two programmers work together at one computer.

One, the driver, types in code while the other, the navigator, gives ideas and feedback. The two coders switch roles frequently.

## Groupwork Debugging Challenge

### `activecode:: challenge1-1`

Working in pairs, debug the following code.

Can you find all the bugs and get the code to run?

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challenge1-1`

```java
public class Challenge1_1
{
   public static void main(String[] args)
   {
       System.out.print("Good morning! ")
       system.out.print("Good afternoon!);
       System.Print " And good evening!";

}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: challenge1-1`

Runestone expects output from `main` containing:

```text
Good morning! Good afternoon! And good evening
```

The classroom task is to fix all syntax errors while preserving the three greetings.

## Error Type Check

### Compile-time, run-time, logic, comment

::: {.tight-list}
- A <span class="term">compile-time error</span> is detected by the compiler.
- A <span class="term">run-time error</span> happens during execution after compilation.
- A <span class="term">logic error</span> causes incorrect behavior.
- A <span class="term">comment</span> is skipped by the compiler and read by humans.
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define <span class="term">run-time error</span> as an error during execution
- explain that an <span class="term">exception</span> interrupts normal program flow
- identify `3/0` as the operation that causes an `ArithmeticException`
- distinguish run-time errors from logic errors
- write `//` and `/* */` comments correctly
- use pair programming roles to debug the challenge code
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
